questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the idiom Memory like sieve
questions.explanation: B) Memory like sieve(idiom) – Poor memory खराब स्मृति

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Assiduous
questions.explanation: C) Assiduous– Diligent, persevering, industrious, attentive, tireless, hard-working परिश्रमी Antonym: Idle – Lazy, indolent, shiftless, sluggish आलसी

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. It’s funny how every time he fails to apply the _________ and _________ the glass.
questions.explanation: B) Brakes'का उपयोग पहले खाली स्थान में होगा क्योंकि "apply the brakes" का अर्थ होता है वाहन या किसी मशीन को रोकने के लिए ब्रेक का उपयोग करना। यह संदर्भ में सही बैठता है क्योंकि व्यक्ति ब्रेक लगाने में विफल हो रहा है। दूसरे खाली स्थान में 'breaks' का उपयोग होगा क्योंकि कांच टूट जाता है, और 'breaks' का अर्थ होता है टूटना या दरार पड़ना। 'Brakes'will be used in the first blank because "apply the brakes" refers to using brakes to stop a vehicle or machine. This fits the context where the person fails to apply the brakes. 'Breaks' will be used in the second blank because the glass breaks, meaning it shatters or cracks.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. The candidate blamed the persistent traffic jam as reason of his being late and the interviewer believed him though he was not convinced.
options: thought err is human and to forgive is divine
did not judge the book by its cover
questions.explanation: A)Gave the benefit of doubt(idiom) - Accepting someone's statement or explanation despite having doubts about its veracity. संदेह का लाभ देना

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. In the vast tapestry of human history, local histories are the threads that give texture and depth to the narrative. B. These stories, often overlooked in favour of grander national or global histories, are essential in shaping the identities of communities. C. Local histories encompass the unique experiences, traditions, and events of specific communities. D. By preserving and promoting local histories, we can foster a sense of belonging, pride, and continuity among community members.
questions.explanation: D) ABDC A: This is a good introductory sentence because it establishes the main idea of the paragraph—how local histories contribute to the larger historical narrative. B: It uses the word "These stories," referring back to the "local histories" in A, which creates a connection between the two sentences. Without A, the pronoun "These" would not have a clear antecedent. D: Sentence D discusses the impact of preserving and promoting local histories. This gives a logical progression after the importance of local histories has been explained in B. C: C defines local histories in more specific terms, concluding the paragraph with details.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Punishment inflicted on someone as vengeance for a wrong or criminal act.
questions.explanation: D) Retribution(noun) – Punishment inflicted on someone as vengeance for a wrong or criminal act प्रतिशोध

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. The university accorded him an honorary degree for his contributions to science.
questions.explanation: D) Accord(to) (verb) – Grant, Give, Allocate, Assign, Bestow प्रदान करना Synonym: Grant(verb) – Allow, provide, give, accord. प्रदान करना Withhold(verb) – Retain, hold back, reserve, keep रोकना Refuse(verb) – Deny, reject, decline, spurn अस्वीकार करना Deny(verb) – Refuse, reject, disallow, negate इनकार करना

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. The doctrine that all people are equal and deserve equal rights and opportunities
questions.explanation: B)Egalitarianism(noun) – The doctrine that all people are equal and deserve equal rights and opportunities. समानतावाद

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and complete the expression. I begin to pronounce the sequence of words and numbers that will prevent her from giving him a piece of her ____________.
questions.explanation: B)Mind(noun) – Used in the phrase "give someone a piece of one's mind", which means to express one's anger or disapproval towards someone openly and strongly. डाँटना या फटकार लगाना Cake(noun) – A sweet baked food made from flour, sugar, and other ingredients. केक Art(noun) – The expression or application of human creative skill and imagination. कला Music(noun) – Vocal or instrumental sounds combined in such a way as to produce harmony and expression of emotion. संगीत

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: A)'Aristocrate'के बदले 'Aristocrat' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि wrong spelling है; जैसे— He is considered an aristocrat in his community.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence Due to budget constraints, the management decided to put the expansion project on hold until a more favorable time.
questions.explanation: B)Shelve(verb) – To decide not to proceed with a project temporarily स्थगित करना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Consummate
questions.explanation: D) Consummate– Perfect, excellent, ideal, flawless, supreme, complete परिपूर्ण, परम Antonym:Inept– Incompetent, inexpert, clumsy अयोग्य

questions.question: Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the given sentence. The board reviewing the courses offered by the afiliated college found that the quality of academic programs was usually good but somewhat irregular.
questions.explanation: D) 'afiliated' की जगह'affiliated'का प्रयोग होगा, क्योंकि 'affiliated' सही spelling है। affiliated' will be used instead of 'afiliated' because 'affiliated' is the correctly spelled word.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. Perseverance is key to overcoming obstacles in both personal and professional life.
questions.explanation: D) Perseverance(noun) – Persistence, Determination, Tenacity, Endurance, Resolve दृढ़ता Synonym: Determination(noun) – Resolution, resolve, steadfastness, firmness दृढ़ संकल्प Indifference(noun) – Apathy, unconcern, disinterest, detachment उदासीनता Negligence(noun) – Carelessness, neglect, oversight, disregard लापरवाही Inconsistency(noun) – Variability, irregularity, fluctuation, contradiction असंगति

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. I sat down opposite him(A)/and produced(B)/the packet of cigarettes.(C)/no error(D)
questions.explanation: B) ‘produced’के बदले ‘offered him’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि ‘produce’ का सामान्यतः अर्थ होता है ‘पैदा करना, उत्पन्न करना, प्रस्तुत करना’ जबकि ‘offer’ का अर्थ होता है ‘अर्पित करना’A प्रस्तुत प्रश्न में ‘सिगरेट का पैकेट बढ़ाया’ का भाव है; अर्थात् ‘offer’ किया का भाव है। B) 'offered him' will be used instead of 'produced' because 'produce' usually means 'to produce, produce, present' whereas 'offer' means 'to offer' The question has the meaning of 'offered cigarette packet'.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. I don’t buy it
options: Only good decisions can be agreed on
I do not like to buy
questions.explanation: C)I don’t buy it(phrase) – I am not convinced मैं आश्वस्त नहीं हूँ

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. His ostensible reason for visiting was to check on the project, but he seemed more interested in the upcoming corporate election.
questions.explanation: B) Ostensible(adjective) – Apparent, Seeming, Supposed, Superficial, Alleged प्रत्यक्ष Synonym: Apparent(adjective) – Obvious, clear, evident, manifest, plain स्पष्ट Empower(verb) – Enable, authorize, strengthen, permit, capacitate सशक्त बनाना Generic(adjective) – Non-branded, universal, common, standard, general सामान्य Disproportionate(adjective) – Unequal, unbalanced, inordinate, excessive, unreasonable असमान

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The difference between successful person/and others is not/a lack of knowledge/but a lack of will.
questions.explanation: A) ‘successful’के पहले ‘article ‘A’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि ‘successful’ एक adjective है और इसके बाद ‘person’ एक singular countable noun है। अतः सही प्रयोग ‘ a successful person’ होगा। noun ‘person’ को यदि plural बना दिया जाए तब भी वाक्य शुद्ध हो जाएगा। अर्थात् ‘a successful person’ के बदले ‘successful persons’ का प्रयोग भी किया जा सकता है। A)The article 'A' will be used before 'successful' because 'successful' is an adjective and after 'person' is a singular countable noun. Hence the correct usage would be 'a successful person'. If the noun 'person' is made plural, then the sentence will be correct. That is, 'successful persons' can also be used instead of 'a successful person'.

questions.question: Improve the bold part in each of the following sentences, if needed. Their all hard work had been of no use.
questions.explanation: A) ‘Their all' के बदले 'All their’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योकि ‘All + Possessive Adjective’ का प्रयोग होता है; जैसे- A)'All their' will be used instead of 'Their all' because 'All + Possessive Adjective' is used; like-

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. Social media was flooded with posts and advertisements, each one more eye-catching than the other. Q. But among these, a campaign called ‘Leave.EU’ began to stand out. The messages played on fears, hopes, and a sense of lost identity. R. ‘Leave.EU’ harnessed digital platforms to target individuals with calculated and data-driven content, shaping their perceptions and pushing them towards voting to leave the European Union. S. Imagine a summer evening in the United Kingdom, right before the Brexit referendum.
questions.explanation: A) SPQR Sstarts with an imperative verb "Imagine", which sets the tone for the reader to visualize a scenario. It introduces the context and thus fits naturally as the opening sentence. P follows S because it describes the situation ("Social media was flooded"), which complements the scene set in S. The past tense "was flooded" fits grammatically and contextually as a description of the scenario after introducing the Brexit period. Q uses the contrasting conjunction "But", which connects directly to the description of social media in P. The conjunction indicates a transition to something specific within the broader situation, making it follow logically after P Sentence R gives further details about the 'Leave.EU' campaign introduced in Sentence Q.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. As Paris is ready to host its third Olympics after a century, the 117 Indian athletes are _____1_____earnestly to increase the country’s medal count in the post-COVID Games, kicking off on the banks of the ______2_____ river Seine on July 26. When the pandemic-delayed Olympics were staged behind closed doors in Tokyo three years ago, India claimed seven medals to record its best-ever tally. With the country aiming to bid for the 2036 Games, it is understandable that the government, pumping in money into sports promotion, _______3______optimistic about double-digit medals. World and Olympic champion javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra is the brightest medal contender for India. The second Indian individual gold medallist after shooter Abhinav Bindra, he is fourth among the leading throwers this season with a mark of 88.36m. To _____4______ his legendary status, he must perform at his peak and overcome several elite competitors. Shuttler P.V. Sindhu, the only Indian woman with two Olympic medals, will seek to overcome a slump and bag an ______5_______third medal. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: C) 'gearing up'का use होगा क्योंकि "are" के साथ main verb के रूप में present continuous tense उपयुक्त है। यहाँ खिलाड़ी अभी तैयारी की प्रक्रिया में हैं। जबकि 'gears up' singular है और मुख्य रूप से present simple tense में अकेले प्रयोग होता है जो कि बहुवचन Subject "athletes" के साथ और "are" के साथ मेल नहीं खाता। 'Geared up' past tense में है लेकिन यहाँ भविष्य की तैयारी की बात हो रही है। 'Gear up' infinitive रूप में है जो यहाँ आवश्यक नहीं है। 'Gearing up' will be used because it matches the plural subject "athletes" with the auxiliary verb "are" to form the present continuous tense, showing an ongoing preparation process. ‘Gears up’ is singular and does not fit with plural “athletes,” ‘Geared up’ implies past tense not suitable for an ongoing process, and ‘Gear up’ is an infinitive form that doesn’t fit the sentence structure.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. As Paris is ready to host its third Olympics after a century, the 117 Indian athletes are _____1_____earnestly to increase the country’s medal count in the post-COVID Games, kicking off on the banks of the ______2_____ river Seine on July 26. When the pandemic-delayed Olympics were staged behind closed doors in Tokyo three years ago, India claimed seven medals to record its best-ever tally. With the country aiming to bid for the 2036 Games, it is understandable that the government, pumping in money into sports promotion, _______3______optimistic about double-digit medals. World and Olympic champion javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra is the brightest medal contender for India. The second Indian individual gold medallist after shooter Abhinav Bindra, he is fourth among the leading throwers this season with a mark of 88.36m. To _____4______ his legendary status, he must perform at his peak and overcome several elite competitors. Shuttler P.V. Sindhu, the only Indian woman with two Olympic medals, will seek to overcome a slump and bag an ______5_______third medal. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: C) 'Majestic' का use होगा क्योंकि "majestic" का अर्थ होता है भव्य, शानदार, या प्रभावशाली। वाक्य में "banks of the majestic river Seine" का उपयोग यह दर्शाने के लिए किया गया है कि Seine नदी अपनी सुंदरता और भव्यता के लिए जानी जाती है, जो पेरिस में होने वाले ओलंपिक का वातावरण और भी आकर्षक बनाती है। जबकि 'Empathetic' का अर्थ है सहानुभूति रखने वाला, जो नदी के संदर्भ में अप्रासंगिक है। 'Armistice' का अर्थ है युद्धविराम, जो यहाँ नदी का वर्णन करने के लिए उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'Gigantic' का अर्थ है विशाल, जो नदी की भव्यता और ऐतिहासिक महत्व को पूरी तरह व्यक्त नहीं करता। 'Majestic' will be used because it means grand, magnificent, or impressive. The phrase "banks of the majestic river Seine" highlights the elegance and grandeur of the river, enhancing the context of the Paris Olympics. On the other hand, 'Empathetic' means showing sympathy, which is irrelevant to describing a river. 'Armistice' means truce, which does not fit here. 'Gigantic' only describes enormous size, failing to capture the river's elegance and historical significance.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. As Paris is ready to host its third Olympics after a century, the 117 Indian athletes are _____1_____earnestly to increase the country’s medal count in the post-COVID Games, kicking off on the banks of the ______2_____ river Seine on July 26. When the pandemic-delayed Olympics were staged behind closed doors in Tokyo three years ago, India claimed seven medals to record its best-ever tally. With the country aiming to bid for the 2036 Games, it is understandable that the government, pumping in money into sports promotion, _______3______optimistic about double-digit medals. World and Olympic champion javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra is the brightest medal contender for India. The second Indian individual gold medallist after shooter Abhinav Bindra, he is fourth among the leading throwers this season with a mark of 88.36m. To _____4______ his legendary status, he must perform at his peak and overcome several elite competitors. Shuttler P.V. Sindhu, the only Indian woman with two Olympic medals, will seek to overcome a slump and bag an ______5_______third medal. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: C) 'Is' का use होगा क्योंकि sentence present tense में है और यह बताता है कि सरकार अभी भी खेलों में पदकों की संख्या बढ़ाने को लेकर आशान्वित है। 'Is' यहाँ सरकार (government) के लिए सही singular verb है। 'Are' plural के लिए use होता है, जो singular subject 'government' के साथ मेल नहीं खाता। 'Was' भूतकाल (past tense) का उपयोग करता है, जो वर्तमान में सरकार की आशावादिता को नहीं दर्शा सकता। 'Were' plural और past tense दोनों के लिए use होता है, जो यहाँ गलत है। • 'Is' will be used because the sentence is in the present tense and describes the government’s current optimism about winning medals. 'Is' is the correct singular verb for the singular subject 'government.' 'Are' is used for plural subjects, which does not align with the singular subject 'government. 'Was' is past tense and cannot convey the government’s ongoing optimism. 'Were' is for plural subjects or hypothetical past situations, which is inappropriate here.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. As Paris is ready to host its third Olympics after a century, the 117 Indian athletes are _____1_____earnestly to increase the country’s medal count in the post-COVID Games, kicking off on the banks of the ______2_____ river Seine on July 26. When the pandemic-delayed Olympics were staged behind closed doors in Tokyo three years ago, India claimed seven medals to record its best-ever tally. With the country aiming to bid for the 2036 Games, it is understandable that the government, pumping in money into sports promotion, _______3______optimistic about double-digit medals. World and Olympic champion javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra is the brightest medal contender for India. The second Indian individual gold medallist after shooter Abhinav Bindra, he is fourth among the leading throwers this season with a mark of 88.36m. To _____4______ his legendary status, he must perform at his peak and overcome several elite competitors. Shuttler P.V. Sindhu, the only Indian woman with two Olympic medals, will seek to overcome a slump and bag an ______5_______third medal. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4
questions.explanation: D) 'Bolster'का use होगा क्योंकि "bolster" का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ को मज़बूत करना या समर्थन देना। sentence में नीरज चोपड़ा को अपनी "legendary status" को मज़बूत करने के लिए अपनी श्रेष्ठता साबित करनी होगी। "Bolster" यहाँ सही विकल्प है क्योंकि यह उनके प्रदर्शन को और अधिक मजबूती देने के भाव को दर्शाता है। जबकि 'Resolve' का अर्थ है समस्या का समाधान या दृढ़ता दिखाना, 'Cater' का अर्थ है सेवा प्रदान करना, और 'Deter' का अर्थ है रोकना या हतोत्साहित करना। 'Bolster' will be used because it means to strengthen or support something. In the sentence, Neeraj Chopra needs to reinforce his legendary status by performing at his peak and overcoming elite competitors. 'Bolster' fits perfectly as it signifies adding strength. On the other hand, 'Resolve' means to solve or show determination, 'Cater' means to provide a service, and 'Deter' means to discourage or prevent.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. As Paris is ready to host its third Olympics after a century, the 117 Indian athletes are _____1_____earnestly to increase the country’s medal count in the post-COVID Games, kicking off on the banks of the ______2_____ river Seine on July 26. When the pandemic-delayed Olympics were staged behind closed doors in Tokyo three years ago, India claimed seven medals to record its best-ever tally. With the country aiming to bid for the 2036 Games, it is understandable that the government, pumping in money into sports promotion, _______3______optimistic about double-digit medals. World and Olympic champion javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra is the brightest medal contender for India. The second Indian individual gold medallist after shooter Abhinav Bindra, he is fourth among the leading throwers this season with a mark of 88.36m. To _____4______ his legendary status, he must perform at his peak and overcome several elite competitors. Shuttler P.V. Sindhu, the only Indian woman with two Olympic medals, will seek to overcome a slump and bag an ______5_______third medal. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5
questions.explanation: A) 'Unprecedented'का use होगा क्योंकि "unprecedented" का अर्थ है "अभूतपूर्व", यानी पहले कभी ऐसा नहीं हुआ। पी.वी. सिंधु के पास पहले ही दो ओलंपिक पदक हैं, और अगर वह तीसरा पदक जीतती हैं, तो यह भारत की ओर से किसी महिला खिलाड़ी के लिए एक अभूतपूर्व उपलब्धि होगी। इसीलिए ‘unprecedented’ यहाँ सबसे उपयुक्त शब्द है। जबकि 'Obligated' का अर्थ है बाध्य होना, 'Excited' का अर्थ है उत्साहित होना, और 'Assorted' का अर्थ है विविध प्रकार का होना, ये शब्द इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठते। • 'Unprecedented' will be used because it means something that has never happened before. P.V. Sindhu already has two Olympic medals, and winning a third would be an extraordinary feat never achieved by any other Indian woman athlete, making 'unprecedented' the right choice. Meanwhile, 'Obligated' means compelled, 'Excited' means feeling eager or enthusiastic, and 'Assorted' means of various kinds. These do not fit the context of describing a never-before-seen accomplishment.
